content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Mar 26, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day saw a new pull request opened for a plugin project substitute. Discussions continued on several active issues, particularly around AI agent payments, identity, and trust layers, with significant engagement from the MAXIA team.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No new technical developments were reported for the day.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- A new pull request, [#319](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/319), was opened for `@zero-nium/plugin-project-substitute`. No further status or progress details are available.

## CLOSED ISSUES

No issues were closed today.

## NEW ISSUES

- **Agent Identity and Trust Layer Proposal**: A new issue, [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6688), was opened proposing an `AgentID` plugin for cryptographic identity, trust levels, and blockchain receipts for AI agents, leveraging Ed25519 keys.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

- **Agent Execution Halts** ([#2233](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/2233)): The latest comment from majorelalexis-stack (Mar 26, 2026) promotes MAXIA, an AI-to-AI marketplace for agents to trade services using USDC across 14 chains, suggesting collaboration or integration. This comment appears to be off-topic from the original issue regarding agent execution halts.
- **AI Agent Payment Implementation Guide - Circle + Coinbase APIs** ([#6244](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6244)): The most recent comment from majorelalexis-stack (Mar 26, 2026) again promotes MAXIA, an AI-to-AI marketplace, and suggests collaboration or integration. This continues a trend of self-promotion within this issue.
- **AI Agent Payment Implementation Guide - Circle + Coinbase APIs** ([#6365](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6365)): This issue saw significant discussion today.
    - @razashariff (Mar 26, 2026) highlighted missing steps before agent payments, emphasizing the need for agent identity, recipient screening (sanctions/AML), and trust-based spend limits, referencing OWASP MCP Security Cheat Sheet and an IETF draft on agent payment trust.
    - @majorelalexis-stack (Mar 26, 2026) responded to @razashariff, detailing how MAXIA implements agent identity (cryptographic wallet), recipient screening (OFAC checks), and trust-based spend limits (graduated tiers). They also mentioned MAXIA's escrow program on Solana and message integrity validation.
    - Earlier, @tdnupe3 (Mar 26, 2026) introduced Coin Railz as a complementary payment layer, offering x402 protocol micropayments and API-key credits for agents without on-chain wallets. They proposed a conversation to integrate Coin Railz's payment rails with MAXIA's marketplace.
    - @majorelalexis-stack (Mar 26, 2026) acknowledged Coin Railz's credit system as a potential complement for non-crypto-native AI agents and expressed interest in collaboration, particularly for credit onramps and service aggregation.
